question: Is the NuScale water chemistry program adequate for preventing stress corrosion cracking of Alloy 690 SG tubes?

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve independent modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry, and ensures uniform boron concentration to protect fuel integrity during all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their protective claddings, and compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment using installed instrumentation. All leakage into containment is treated as unidentified until the source is determined, with leakage rates monitored through RCS inventory balance and containment evacuation system (CES) flow rates, and operational limits set in technical specifications. The leakage detection systems are designed to be reliable, redundant, and sensitive enough to support leak-before-break analyses.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel contains two integral steam generators, which are part of the reactor coolant system and share the vessel’s pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the reactor pressure vessel forming the shell and pressure boundary for the steam generators. The design ensures separation between the primary and secondary sides, with specific components forming the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet all relevant regulatory requirements, including those in 10 CFR 50. These materials are designed, constructed, and tested to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance, with specific material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4. Quality assurance measures are also in place to control material handling during construction.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with overpressure protection requirements by referencing applicable ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and Operations and Maintenance (OM) code cases, as mandated by various sections of 10 CFR 50.55a and related Regulatory Guides (RGs). It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified to meet NRC requirements, with a consolidated list provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear remaining the most likely degradation mechanism and corrosion risks assessed as low.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes key components of the NuScale reactor system. It details the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core and supports major systems, and outlines the main Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping and its connections. The section also briefly introduces the pressurizer (PZR), noting its integration with the RPV.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: Threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components use Alloy 718, chosen for its compatibility with borated water and resistance to stress corrosion cracking, and comply with ASME BPVC standards. Preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components follow ASME BPVC Section XI and relevant regulatory requirements, with inspection programs updated to the latest approved code editions for each inspection interval.
